title: Fun Run

jokes.text: Jan made me breakfast this morning. Well, she bought the milk. It's soy.
jokes.setup: Michael claims Jan made him breakfast, suggesting domestic partnership
jokes.punchline: She only bought milk, and it's soy milk - minimal effort
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Michael's self-delusion about his relationship, economically structured with good misdirection

jokes.text: This is why I do it. That's what I have to come home to.
jokes.setup: Michael just described minimal domestic effort from Jan
jokes.punchline: He presents this as motivation and something wonderful to come home to
jokes.explanation: Strong character-driven irony showing Michael's complete self-delusion about his relationship situation

jokes.text: What kind of celebrity? It's not relevant. How much did you pay for it? Not relevant. You paid for it? It all happened so fast.
jokes.setup: Jim questioning Dwight about internet offers
jokes.punchline: Dwight paid for celebrity sex tape, gets increasingly defensive
jokes.explanation: Classic cringe escalation as Dwight digs himself deeper, good rhythm in the defensive responses

jokes.text: Are you kidding me? Pam and Jim are totally hooking up. All they do is smile.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Jim and Pam's relationship status
jokes.punchline: Kelly's evidence is simply that they smile - oversimplified logic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kelly character moment - jumping to conclusions with flimsy evidence delivered with complete confidence

jokes.text: I took her to the hospital and the doctors tried to save her life. They did the best that they could and she is going to be okay.
jokes.setup: Michael announces Meredith was hit by a car, speaks as if she died
jokes.punchline: After building dramatic tension, reveals she's actually fine
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection structure - Michael's dramatic phrasing makes everyone think she died, classic Michael obliviousness

jokes.text: What is wrong with you? Why did you have to phrase it like that?
jokes.setup: Michael's misleading announcement about Meredith
jokes.punchline: Jim voices what everyone is thinking about Michael's terrible communication
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man response, Jim as voice of reason calling out Michael's oblivious communication style

jokes.text: She has a slight pelvical fracture, but people have survived far worse.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to downplay Meredith's injury
jokes.punchline: Says 'pelvical' instead of 'pelvic' and treats fracture as minor
jokes.explanation: Michael's mispronunciation and casual attitude toward serious injury, good character consistency

jokes.text: I was able to be on the scene so quickly because I was in the car that hit her.
jokes.setup: Michael seems heroic for being first on scene
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was the one who hit her
jokes.explanation: Masterful misdirection - Michael presents himself as hero before revealing he caused the problem. Perfect structure and economy.

jokes.text: Who was driving? [silence] Oh, Michael.
jokes.setup: Jim asks who was driving after Michael's revelation
jokes.punchline: Michael's guilty silence followed by Jim's resigned realization
jokes.explanation: Perfect use of silence and timing, Jim's weary acceptance of Michael's incompetence

jokes.text: One day Michael came in complaining about a speed bump on the highway. I wonder who he ran over then.
jokes.setup: Establishing Michael's history of car incidents
jokes.punchline: Implies Michael has hit people before and called them speed bumps
jokes.explanation: Dark humor connecting Michael's obliviousness to past incidents, excellent character consistency

jokes.text: It's only Meredith. Yeah. It's only Meredith. Thank God.
jokes.setup: Office workers learning about the accident
jokes.punchline: Dismissive attitude because it's Meredith, treating her as expendable
jokes.explanation: Dark workplace humor about hierarchy and expendability, good character moment for Kevin and Stanley's callousness

jokes.text: Was she talking back? Just got sick of that face? Did she owe you money? Is this downsizing? Did she spurn your advances?
jokes.setup: Packer asking why Michael hit Meredith
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous motives, treating it as intentional
jokes.explanation: Perfect Packer character moment - escalating inappropriate suggestions, good comedic rhythm

jokes.text: There's bad blood, jealousies, cliques...
jokes.setup: Jim suggests other cats could keep Sprinkles company
jokes.punchline: Angela describes cat social dynamics like high school drama
jokes.explanation: Perfect Angela character moment - treating cats like complex social beings, absurd but believable for her

jokes.text: So, who is the real boss, the dog or a fish?
jokes.setup: Michael's analogy about him being top dog vs Ryan being little fish
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical comparison that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael logic - confident delivery of complete nonsense, good absurdist character moment

jokes.text: It was on company property with company property, so double jeopardy. We are fine.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining legal implications of hitting Meredith
jokes.punchline: Completely misunderstands 'double jeopardy' legal concept
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael malapropism - confidently wrong about legal concepts, very quotable

jokes.text: I don't think you understand how jeopardy works. Oh, right. I'm sorry. What is 'We're fine'?
jokes.setup: Toby correcting Michael's legal misunderstanding
jokes.punchline: Michael thinks Toby means the game show Jeopardy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay - Michael's misunderstanding leads to perfect game show format response. Excellent structure and timing.

jokes.text: Can you do me a little favor? Go to my place at lunch and give Sprinkles her medicine? Sure. I have to visit the alkie.
jokes.setup: Angela asking Dwight to care for sick cat
jokes.punchline: Dwight casually calls Meredith 'the alkie'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight callousness - casual dehumanization delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Roll the insulin in your hands. Don't shake it... Mix one capsule of omega fatty acid in with her kidney medicine... there's a fungal cream because she has this infection under her tail...
jokes.setup: Angela giving Dwight instructions for cat care
jokes.punchline: Increasingly complex medical regimen for a cat, ending with gross tail infection detail
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Angela's obsessive cat care, builds to gross-out punchline

jokes.text: Guess what? I have flaws. What are they? Oh, I don't know. I sing in the shower. Sometimes, I spend too much time volunteering. Occasionally, I'll hit somebody with my car. So sue me.
jokes.setup: Michael admitting he has flaws
jokes.punchline: Lists humble brags then casually includes hitting people with cars
jokes.explanation: Masterful escalation - Michael's 'flaws' are humble brags until the shocking casual admission. Perfect structure and highly quotable.

jokes.text: No, don't sue me. That's the opposite of the point that I'm trying to make.
jokes.setup: Michael just said 'so sue me' about hitting people with cars
jokes.punchline: Immediately realizes the legal implications and backtracks
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up to previous joke - Michael's moment of clarity about his poor word choice

jokes.text: She looks like an angel. She looks awful. No? Okay, she always looks like that.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to say something nice about unconscious Meredith
jokes.punchline: Accidentally insults her normal appearance when corrected
jokes.explanation: Good structure showing Michael's inability to say the right thing, accidental cruelty

jokes.text: Oh, really? What kind? Codeine? Vicodin? Percocet? Fentanyl? OxyContin? Palladone? What? I have no idea.
jokes.setup: Meredith mentions being on painkillers
jokes.punchline: Creed rattles off specific drug names with suspicious knowledge
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed character moment - suspiciously specific knowledge of drugs, delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Forgiveness is next to godliness.
jokes.setup: Michael quoting the Bible about forgiveness
jokes.punchline: Mangles the phrase 'cleanliness is next to godliness'
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael malapropism - confident misstatement of common phrase

jokes.text: Do I need to be liked? Absolutely not. I like to be liked. I enjoy being liked. I have to be liked, but it's not like this compulsive need to be liked, like my need to be praised.
jokes.setup: Michael claiming he doesn't need to be liked
jokes.punchline: Escalating admissions that completely contradict his opening claim
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Michael's complete lack of self-awareness, builds perfectly to the praise punchline

jokes.text: Any problems? Well, you left the TV on, and your cat is dead.
jokes.setup: Angela asking if there were problems with cat-sitting
jokes.punchline: Dwight casually mentions the cat died like a minor inconvenience
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery - Dwight treats pet death as equivalent to leaving TV on. Excellent economy and timing.

jokes.text: So... Hey, come on, don't be sad. Just... Okay? Just... She's in a better place. All right. Actually, the place that she's in is the freezer because of the odor.
jokes.setup: Dwight offering comfort with clichéd 'better place' line
jokes.punchline: Reveals the 'better place' is literally the freezer
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - takes common comforting phrase and makes it literal and horrible

jokes.text: Prinkles! God... That's three things. I'll tell you what's going on. This office is cursed.
jokes.setup: Michael listing recent office tragedies
jokes.punchline: Calls cat 'Prinkles' instead of 'Sprinkles', declares office cursed
jokes.explanation: Michael's mispronunciation followed by superstitious conclusion, good character consistency

jokes.text: I'm not superstitious, but I am a little stitious.
jokes.setup: Michael discussing his beliefs about the curse
jokes.punchline: Creates nonsense word 'stitious' by removing prefix
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay - Michael's pseudo-logical creation of new word is perfectly economical and highly quotable

jokes.text: Cats do not provide milk or wool or meat.
jokes.setup: Dwight responding to Angela's grief over her cat
jokes.punchline: Reduces cats to purely utilitarian farm animal standards
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight logic - applies farm practical mindset to pets, brutally economical

jokes.text: I've been involved in a number of cults, both as a leader and a follower. You have more fun as a follower. But you make more money as a leader.
jokes.setup: Discussing religious beliefs
jokes.punchline: Creed casually mentions cult involvement with business analysis
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed moment - shocking admission delivered with practical business advice. Dark and highly quotable.

jokes.text: Well, if you're going to reduce my identity to my religion, then I'm Sikh. But I also like hip-hop and NPR, and I'm restoring a 1967 Corvette in my spare time.
jokes.setup: Michael asking about religious beliefs
jokes.punchline: Ryan provides overly detailed identity beyond religion
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ryan's pretentiousness and need to seem complex

jokes.text: As a farmer, I know that when an animal is sick, sometimes the right thing to do is to put it out of its misery. With the electricity we're using to keep Meredith alive, we could power a small fan for two days.
jokes.setup: Dwight discussing care for sick animals
jokes.punchline: Applies euthanasia logic to Meredith, with oddly specific alternative
jokes.explanation: Dark Dwight logic applied to humans, the specific fan detail makes it absurd and memorable

jokes.text: Your last tetanus shot was when you were bitten by a bat a few months ago? Yes. Dwight, here, trapped it in a bag against my head.
jokes.setup: Doctor reviewing Meredith's medical history
jokes.punchline: Reveals Dwight caused the bat bite incident
jokes.explanation: Good callback revealing Dwight's involvement, escalates his danger to Meredith

jokes.text: Maybe there's some sort of animal that we could make a sacrifice to, like a giant buffalo, or some sort of monster, like something with the body of a walrus with the head of a sea lion.
jokes.setup: Michael looking for alternatives to God
jokes.punchline: Increasingly bizarre mythical creatures for sacrifice
jokes.explanation: Michael's absurd logic creating increasingly specific mythical creatures, good escalation

jokes.text: But it may make you feel a little bit better to know that before that happened, Dwight endangered her life by putting a garbage bag over her head that had a bat in it.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to deflect blame for hitting Meredith
jokes.punchline: Reveals Dwight's equally dangerous bat incident
jokes.explanation: Good callback used for deflection, escalates the absurdity of office dangers

jokes.text: Thanks to me, she went to the hospital and that saved her life.
jokes.setup: Michael discussing Meredith's rabies exposure
jokes.punchline: Claims hitting her with car was actually saving her life
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael self-serving logic - turning his mistake into heroism, great character moment

jokes.text: Is there a God? If not, what are all these churches for? And who is Jesus' dad?
jokes.setup: Michael pondering existence of God
jokes.punchline: Naive questions about basic Christian theology
jokes.explanation: Michael's childlike understanding of religion, simple but character-consistent

jokes.text: I thought they'd be good together, like PB and J. Pam Beasley and Jim.
jokes.setup: Kevin discussing Jim and Pam's relationship
jokes.punchline: Makes connection between PB&J sandwich and their initials
jokes.explanation: Sweet Kevin moment making innocent connection, good wordplay and character consistency

jokes.text: Almost marrying Roy Anderson was as close to Pamela Anderson as I ever want to be.
jokes.setup: Pam discussing her past relationship
jokes.punchline: Clever wordplay on similar names Anderson/Anderson
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay showing Pam's wit, excellent economy and structure

jokes.text: A woman shouldn't have to be hit by a car to learn that she may have rabies, but that is where we are in America.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining his fun run motivation
jokes.punchline: Treats absurd specific situation as broader American problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael grandiosity - making personal mistake into societal commentary

jokes.text: Well, back in olden times, a large, fat person like this was a person of power. A person who had money, could buy food, a person of respect, like the regional manager of the day.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to motivate Stanley to run
jokes.punchline: Compares himself to powerful fat person of olden times
jokes.explanation: Michael's attempt at motivation backfires, good character moment showing his tone-deafness

jokes.text: I'm petrified of nipple chafing. Once it starts, it is a vicious circle. You have sensitive nipples, they chafe. So they become more sensitive, so they chafe more.
jokes.setup: Andy preparing for the run
jokes.punchline: Overshares detailed theory about nipple chafing cycle
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy oversharing, uncomfortable topic delivered with scientific analysis

jokes.text: When I got home, Sprinkles' body was in the freezer where Dwight said he left her. But all my bags of frozen French fries had been clawed to shreds.
jokes.setup: Angela investigating her cat's death
jokes.punchline: Evidence suggests cat was alive when put in freezer
jokes.explanation: Dark revelation with detective work, builds mystery and horror of the situation

jokes.text: What's your strategy for this race? Well, I'm going to start fast. Then I'm going to run fast in the middle. Then I'm going to end fast. Why won't more people do that? 'Cause they're stupid.
jokes.setup: Jim asking about Dwight's race strategy
jokes.punchline: Dwight's completely naive strategy and confidence in his logic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight logic - oversimplified solution delivered with complete confidence

jokes.text: Right. ...we're dating. Wow! There it is. Yeah. We haven't told anybody, but it's going really great.
jokes.setup: Jim and Pam caught looking romantic
jokes.punchline: Finally admit they're dating after trying to hide it
jokes.explanation: Major character moment, payoff to episode-long speculation, high emotional impact

jokes.text: Bat birth control? Wait, this money is going to bat birth control, right, Michael?
jokes.setup: Discussion about where charity money goes
jokes.punchline: Reveals Michael's confused understanding of rabies prevention
jokes.explanation: Michael's absurd misunderstanding of rabies control, shows his lack of research

jokes.text: Have you met that kid? Not going to college.
jokes.setup: Suggestion to set up college fund for Meredith's son
jokes.punchline: Michael's brutal assessment delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Michael's casual cruelty delivered with perfect economy, dark but believable

jokes.text: So I closed the door but the image of his... Baguette? ...dangling participle still burned in my eyes.
jokes.setup: Pam describing seeing Michael naked
jokes.punchline: Jim's food euphemism and grammar pun for genitals
jokes.explanation: Clever double entendre with 'dangling participle' - smart wordplay from Jim

jokes.text: They say if you're nervous around someone you should picture them naked. I do not recommend this strategy. Try picturing them with more clothes on or a funny coat.
jokes.setup: Common advice about picturing people naked
jokes.punchline: Pam's traumatized reversal recommending more clothing
jokes.explanation: Great callback with practical alternative advice, well-structured and quotable

jokes.text: Myth: three Americans every year die from rabies. Fact: four Americans every year die from rabies.
jokes.setup: Michael presenting important rabies facts
jokes.punchline: Minimal difference between 'myth' and 'fact'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael logic - dramatic presentation format for trivial difference

jokes.text: I'm like Forrest Gump, except I am not an idiot.
jokes.setup: Michael comparing himself to fictional runner
jokes.punchline: Claims he's not an idiot while consistently proving otherwise
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic self-assessment from Michael, economical and character-perfect

jokes.text: Excellent! Simpsons. Wait, Imodium or Ex-Lax?
jokes.setup: Dwight celebrating putting something in Toby's coffee
jokes.punchline: Realizes he may have used wrong drug with opposite effect
jokes.explanation: Good escalation as Dwight's sabotage potentially backfires spectacularly

jokes.text: You did kill her? I sang her her favorite songs. You put her in my freezer. It was beautiful and gentle and respectful.
jokes.setup: Angela discovering Dwight killed her cat
jokes.punchline: Dwight's proud description of his 'mercy killing'
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe as Dwight describes his well-intentioned but horrible act

jokes.text: Cat heaven is a beautiful place, but you don't get there if you're euthanized.
jokes.setup: Angela processing her cat's death
jokes.punchline: Specific theological rules about cat afterlife
jokes.explanation: Perfect Angela logic - applying religious doctrine to cats with complete seriousness

jokes.text: I know you, Michael. I saw you naked. You don't... You don't know me. You've just seen my penis.
jokes.setup: Pam claiming to know Michael after seeing him naked
jokes.punchline: Michael's distinction between knowing someone and seeing genitals
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Michael's surprisingly logical distinction, economical and memorable

jokes.text: And while I eventually puked my guts out, I never puked my heart out. I'm very, very proud of that.
jokes.setup: Michael describing his race completion
jokes.punchline: Proud distinction between literal and metaphorical 'puking out'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay showing Michael's strange sense of pride and accomplishment

tmdb_overview: Michael comes to believe the office might be cursed in the wake of a freak accident. After scrutinizing each of his staffers' religious beliefs, he organizes a charity 5K "fun run."
